questions.stem: Which expression is equivalent to \(4n^3 + 10n^3 + 4n^2\)?
questions.explanation:
Combine like terms.
$$\begin{aligned}(4n^3+10n^3)+4n^2 &= 14n^3+4n^2\end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: The volume of a tank goes from \(10{,}000\) cubic inches to \(32{,}000\) cubic inches at a constant rate of \(900\) cubic inches per hour. What type of function best models the relationship between the tank's volume and time?
questions.explanation: The volume is increasing by a constant rate, or adding \(900\) cubic inches per hour, which is linear growth, so the function type is increasing linear.

questions.stem: Neja must read at least \(72\) pages per week to finish a certain book before the end of the summer.  Neja reads at a rate of \(6\) pages per hour.  What is the minimum number of hours Neja must read to complete her reading goal for a certain week?
questions.explanation:
Start with \(\frac{72\,\text{pages}}{1\,\text{week}}\). Use unit conversions to calculate the number of hours Neja must read in one week:

$$\begin{aligned}\frac{72\,\cancel{\text{pages}}}{1\,\text{week}} \times \frac{1\,\text{hour}}{6\,\cancel{\text{pages}}} &= \frac{72}{6}\,\text{hours per week} \\ &= 12\,\text{hours per week}\end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: A rectangle has a width of \(23\) inches (in) and a length of \(9\) in.  What is the area of this rectangle?
questions.explanation:
Use the formula for the area of a rectangle, \(A=lw\) where \(l=\) the length of the rectangle and \(w=\) the width of the rectangle.

$$\begin{aligned}A=9(23)=207\end{aligned}$$.

questions.stem:
The values in the data set shown represents the time, in minutes, of several employee's commutes to work.
$$14,1,19,24,3,3,8,16$$
What is the mean time, in minutes, of the commutes?
questions.explanation:
The mean, or average, is found by adding the values of a data set and dividing by the number of values in the data set. There are \(8\) numbers, so divide the sum by \(8\).
$$\begin{aligned}\frac{14+1+19+24+3+3+8+16}{8}=\frac{88}{8}= 11\end{aligned}$$

questions.stem:
$$x^2+14x=32$$
Which of the following is a solution to the given equation?
questions.explanation:
Solve this quadratic equation by setting it equal to zero and factoring.
$$\begin{aligned}x^2+14x-32&=\cancel{32}-\cancel{32}\\x^2+14x-32&=0\end{aligned}$$
Use the Zero Product Property to solve: \(x-2=0\) and \(x+16=0\), so \(x=2\) or \(x=-16\). Of the two solutions, \(x=2\) appears in the answer choices, so D is the correct answer.

This problem can also be solved by substituting answers in for \(x\) and determining whether the equation is true or false. For example:

C) \(4^2+14(4)\neq32\) because \(16+56\neq32\). Incorrect.

D) \(2^2+14(2)=32\) because \(4+28=32\). Correct.

questions.stem: In the figure, line \(l\) is parallel to line \(m\). What is the value of \(y\)?
questions.explanation: The given angle (\(49^\circ\)) is corresponding angles with the angle indicated in the drawing and corresponding angles are congruent on parallel lines. That angle is supplementary angles with \(y^\circ\). Supplementary angles have a sum of \(180^\circ\), so \(y=180-49=131\). The correct answer is C.

questions.stem: Which expression is equivalent to \((a^3 d{}n^7 )(a^2 d^6 n^{-4})\), where \(m\), \(q\), and \(z\) are positive?
questions.explanation:
Use the laws of exponents to simplify \((a^3dn^7)(a^2d^6n^{-4})\). The product rule says that when multiplying powers with the same base, you add the exponents.
$$\begin{aligned}&=(a^3dn^7)(a^2d^6n^{-4})\\ &=a^3a^2d{}d^6n^{7}n^{-4} \\ &=a^{5}d^{7}n^{3}\end{aligned}$$

questions.stem:
$$\sqrt{-3x-17}=\sqrt{(x+5)^2}$$
What is the largest solution to the given equation?
questions.explanation:
Square both sides, so \((\sqrt{-3x-17})^2=(\sqrt{(x+5)^2})^2\) simplifies to \(-3x-17=(x+5)^2\). This is a quadratic equation; expand the right hand side and set the equation equal to zero.
$$\begin{aligned}-3x-17&=(x+5)(x+5)\\ \cancel{-3x}-\cancel{17}+\cancel{3x}+\cancel{17}&=x^2+10x+25+3x-17 \\0&=x^2+13x+42\end{aligned}$$
Determine factors of \(42\) that have a sum of \(13\). The factors are \(6\) and \(7\) because \(6 \times 7=42\) and \(6+7=13\).
$$\begin{aligned}0&=x^2+13x+42 \\0&=(x+6)(x+7)\end{aligned}$$
.
Use the Zero Product Property to solve: \(x+6=0\) and \(x+7=0\), so \(x=-6\) or \(x=-7\). Since this question asks for the largest solution, \(-6\) is the correct answer.

questions.stem: The expression \(\frac{45}{5x+15}\) is equivalent to \(\frac{9}{x+c}\), where \(c\) is a constant and \(x>0\).  What is the value of \(c\)?
questions.explanation: If \(\frac{45}{5x+15}=\frac{9}{x+c}\), it must be the case that the numerator and denominator can be divided by a common factor. Factor the greatest common factor from the denominator, so \(5x+15=5(x+3)\). It's possible that you can see the value of \(c\) from here is \(3\), but if not, simplify the given fraction \(\frac{45}{5(x+3)}\) by dividing the numerator and denominator by \(5\); \(\frac{\cancel{45}}{\cancel{5}(x+3)}=\frac{9}{x+3}\). Since \(\frac{9}{x+c}=\frac{9}{x+3}\), \(c=3\).

questions.stem: The line \(y=kx-8\), where \(k\) is a constant, is graphed in the \(xy-\)plane.  If the point \((a,b)\), where \(a\ne0\) and \(b\ne0\), falls on the line, what is the slope of the line in terms of \(a\) and \(b\)?
questions.explanation:
In the slope-intercept equation \(y=kx-8\), \(k\) is the slope of the equation. Substitute the ordered pair \((a,b)\) in for \((x,y)\) and solve for \(k\):
$$\begin{aligned}y&=kx-8\\b&=ka-8\\b+8&=ka-\cancel{8}+\cancel{8}\\ \frac{b+8}{a}&=\frac{k\cancel{a}}{\cancel{a}}\\ k&=\frac{b+8}{a}\end{aligned}$$

questions.stem:
$$|x+18|=30$$
What is one possible solution to the given equation?
questions.explanation:
The absolute value of a number is its distance from zero. This means that inside the absolute value on the left hand side, \(30\) or \(-30\) will have an absolute value of \(30\), the right hand side of the equation. To solve, write and solve one of two equations; one that gives $30$ or one that gives \(-30\) inside the absolute value:
$$\begin{aligned}x+18 &= 30 \\ x+\cancel{18}-\cancel{18} &= 30-18 \\ x &= 12\end{aligned}$$
$$\begin{aligned}x+18 &= -30 \\ x+\cancel{18}-\cancel{18} &= -30-18 \\ x &= -48\end{aligned}$$

questions.stem:
$$y=15+10x$$
$$y=(x-1)(2x+3)$$
Which ordered pair \((x,y)\) is the solution to the given system of equations?
questions.explanation:
This is a non-linear system of equations. To solve, set the two expressions for \(y\) equal, so \(15+10x=(x-1)(2x+3)\). Expand the right hand side and set the equation equal to zero.
$$\begin{aligned}15+10x&=(x-1)(2x+3)\\ \cancel{15}+\cancel{10x}-\cancel{15}-\cancel{10x}&=2x^2+3x-2x+-3-15-10x\\0&=2x^2-9x-18\end{aligned}$$
To factor by grouping, multiply \(a \times c\). In the expression \(2x^2-9x-18\), \(a=2\) and \(c=-18\), so \(a \times c = -36\). Determine factors of \(-36\) that have a sum of \(-9\). These factors are \(-12\) and \(3\) because \(-12 \times 3= -36\) and \(-12 +3=9\). Re-write the given quadratic expression with these factors.
$$\begin{aligned}&=2x^2-9x-18\\&=2x^2-12x+3x-18\end{aligned}$$
Factor the greatest common factor from \(2x^2-12x\) and \(3x-18\):
$$\begin{aligned}&=2x^2-12x+3x-18\\&=2x(x-6)+3(x-6)\end{aligned}$$
Re-write the expression as the product of two binomials; \(2x(x-6)+3(x-6)=(2x+3)(x-6)\). Use the Zero Product Property to solve: \(2x+3=0\) and \(x+6=0\), so \(x=-\frac{3}{2}\) or \(x=-6\). The answer that appears in the answer choice is \(-\frac{3}{2}\), so D is the correct answer

The other method that can be used to solve this problem is substituting answers; the challenge with this method in this problem is that you will have to substitute a fraction at some point as there are two fractional answer choices.

questions.stem: Which expression is equivalent to the expression \(20m^3 n^3+4m^3 n^6\)?
questions.explanation:
From the answers, notice that this is a factoring problem. Right away you can eliminate C and D because if the terms cannot be combined before factoring, they also cannot be combined after factoring. The greatest common factor, \(4m^3 n^3\), is the same in answer choices A and B so divide both terms by it to find what is remaining inside the parentheses.
$$\begin{aligned}&=\frac{20\cancel{m^3n^3}}{4\cancel{m^3n^3}}\\&= \frac{20}{4}\\&=5\end{aligned}$$
$$\begin{aligned}&=\frac{\cancel{4m^3}n^6}{\cancel{4m^3}n^3}\\&= \frac{n^6}{n^3}\\&=n^3\end{aligned}$$
The factored form of this expression is \(4m^3 n^3 (5+n^3 )\), so the correct answer is B.

questions.stem: Clare rented \(12\) tables that were each the same price. She paid a delivery fee for the tables of \(\$124\). The total cost for the rental including the delivery fee was \(\$220\). What would be the cost, in dollars, to rent one table with no delivery fee?
questions.explanation:
Write and solve an equation to find the cost to rent one table. Let \(t=\) the cost of one table. This means \(12t\) is the cost to rent \(12\) tables. Add the delivery fee to get the total cost, so \(12t+124=220\).
$$\begin{aligned}12t+\cancel{124}-\cancel{124}&=220-124\\ \frac{\cancel{12}t}{\cancel{12}}&=\frac{96}{12}\\t&=8\end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: What is the \(y-\)intercept of the graph shown?
questions.explanation: The \(y\)-intercept is the point where the graph intersects the \(y\)-axis. The \(x\)-value at the \(y\)-intercept is \(0\), so eliminate A and B. In the graph, the \(y\)-intercept crosses the \(y\)-axis at \((0,3)\), so C is the correct answer.

questions.stem: How many times do the graphs of the equations \(y=2x+1\) and \(x+2y=4\) intersect in the \(xy-\)plane?
questions.explanation:
The graphs of linear equations can intersect \(0\), \(1\), or infinitely many times. Eliminate C and D. In general, the rules for how many times lines intersect are as follows:
  • Graphs of equations with the same slope but different \(y\)-intercepts intersect \(0\) times because they are parallel lines.
  • Graphs of equations with different slopes intersect \(1\) time.
  • Graphs of equations with the same slope and same \(y\)-intercept intersect infinitely many times because they are equivalent equations that produce the same line.
To determine how many times the graphs intersect, write the second equation in slope-intercept form, \(y=mx+b\). The first equation is already in slope-intercept form where the slope is \(2\) and the \(y\)-intercept is \(1\).
$$\begin{aligned}\cancel{x}+2y-\cancel{x}&=4-x\\\frac{\cancel{2}y}{\cancel{2}}&=\frac{4-x}{2}\\y&=-\frac{1}{2}x+2\end{aligned}$$
The slope of this line is \(\frac{1}{2}\). Since this is different than the slope of the other line (\(2\)), the lines intersect one time.

questions.stem: What is the value of \(20-4x\) if \(5-x=21\)?
questions.explanation:
To find the value of \(20-4x\) there is no need to solve directly for \(x\). \(20-4x\) is \(5-x\) multiplied by \(4\), so multiply both sides of the given equation by \(4\).
$$\begin{aligned}4(5-x)&=21 \times 4\\20-4x&=84\end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: The legs of a right triangle have lengths of \(32\) inches and \(51\) inches. The length of this hypotenuse of the triangle can be written, in inches, can be written in the form \(5\sqrt{n}\), where \(n\) is an integer, what is the value of \(n\)
questions.explanation:
In order to find the value of \(n\), first find the length of the hypotenuse. Use the Pythagorean Theorem, \(a^2+b^2=c^2\) where the hypotenuse is \(c\).
$$\begin{aligned}32^2+51^2&=c^2\\\sqrt{3,625}&=\sqrt{c^2}\\c&=\sqrt{3,625}\end{aligned}$$
Set the hypotenuse equal to the given length \(5\sqrt{n}\) and solve for \(n\), so \(\sqrt{3,625}=5\sqrt{n}\). Since \(5\) appears as a factor outside the square root, this means it was \(5^2\) inside the radical, so \(\sqrt{5^2 \times 145}=5\sqrt{n}\), and \(n=145\).

questions.stem:
$$x=7y$$
$$-5y+x=28$$
The solution to the given system of equations is \((x,y)\). What is the value of \(y\)?
questions.explanation:
To solve this system of equations, substitute \(7y\) into the second equation for \(x\) and solve for \(y\).
$$\begin{aligned}-5y+x&=28\\-5y+7y&=28\\ \frac{\cancel{2}y}{\cancel{2}}&=\frac{28}{2}\\y&=14\end{aligned}$$

questions.stem:
$$y=a(x+3)(x-5)$$
In the quadratic equation above, \(a\) is a nonzero constant. The graph of the equation in the \(xy-\)plane is a parabola with vertex \((m,n)\). Which of the following is equal to \(n\)?
questions.explanation:
For all parabolas, the \(x\)-value of the vertex is on the axis of symmetry. The axis of symmetry is halfway between pairs of symmetrical points, including the \(x\)-intercepts. In this factored form equation \(y=a(x+3)(x-5)\), the \(x\)-intercepts are \((-3,0)\) and \((5,0)\). To find the axis of symmetry (which is also the \(x\)-value of the vertex), find the average, or midpoint of \(-3\) and \(5\), so \(\frac{-3+5}{2}=1\). Since \((m,n)\) is the vertex, \(m=1\). Substitute \((1,n)\) into the equation to solve for \(n\).
$$\begin{aligned}y&=a(x+3)(x-5)\\n&=a(1+3)(1-5)\\n&=a(4)(-4)\\n&=-16a\end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: Which expression is equivalent to \(x^2-3x-10\)?
questions.explanation:
Method \(1\) Factor. To factor, determine factors of \(-10\) that have a sum of \(-3\). These factors are \(-5\) and \(2\) because \(-5 \times 2= -10\) and \(-5 +2=-3\). Re-write the given quadratic expression with these factors.
$$\begin{aligned}&=x^2-3x-10\\&=(x-5)(x+2)\end{aligned}$$
Method \(2\): Multiply the answer choices to find \(x^2-3x-10\).

A) \((x+5)(x-2)=x^2-2x+5x-10=x^2+3x-10\)Incorrect

B) \((x+10)(x-1)=x^2-x+10x-10=x^2+9x-10\) Incorrect

C) \((x+1)(x-10)=x^2-10x+x-10=x^2-9x-10\) Incorrect

D) \((x+2)(x-5)=x^2-5x+2x-10=x^2+3x-10\) Correct
